reviews.review_description: Yes it is really good i absolutely love this game but they are a few problems some of my settings options are missing and the plobe gravity and real dustance buttons are greyed out for me like half of the features are missing pls fix this idk if its jus my game but i am on standalone meta quest 3s so if u can tell me how to fix this pls do

I remember playing an early version of this for Oculus back in the days (10-12 years ago…?!). Absolutely fell in love with it, and how it made my sense of awe of the solar system and the universe grow immensely. Experiencing how small our huge sun is in comparison to the largest suns is just plain epic. 

BUG REPORT: However, there seems to be a bug with the alternative maps for the planets. I’m only seeing a placeholder image instead of the informative images the guide is talking about. Don’t know if this is just on Quest 3, or a the latest version, or what’s happening. Could the images have disappears from the internet if they are streamed? Seems more likely that they would be stored on disk. Please as the developer, look into this and fix it so I can experience the tour exactly as you intended :)
I have always wanted to explore the solar system in VR and this delivers on so many levels.
Not amazed. But it is good.

What a great app and such value for money. Thoroughly enjoyed visiting and seeing the planets in our solar system and learning so much about them.
It was such an enjoyable experience going through the journey.
Despite of great immersive experience it's hardly possible to grasp this scales. Thank you for your work guys!

￼ This is awesome! You get a tour of the solar system, all planets and moons. The scale is true to life. Being next to the sun and realizing how enormous it is along with getting up close to the planets, being able to reach out and hold them in your hand… The detail is incredible! I recommend this to anyone who has an interest in what is beyond this planet. ￼

best space education vr app out there! you can choose between different tours(short, longer, narrated or not,etc)
every family member or friend i showed this app, were astonished how big space is:))

great app for young and old alike!

EDIT: I never expected to get such a quick response, let alone a fix for what i considered to be a bug. I think the new effect is better and looks much more intentional then the first one.  Thanks a lot!

Original post: "I have had this app for many years now and it's amazing. Then a few days ago i noticed there was an update, which i did not expect, because this app has not gotten an update in a long time. So i started it to find out what changed. Sadly the only thing different that i noticed is that the narrator now has graphical glitches that i had never experienced before. I had this app since the Quest 2 and i'm now on Quest 3. I know that i have tested it on Quest 3 before and did not have that problem. I hope it gets fixed soon."

reviews.review_title: Sorry Elon I got there first.
reviews.review_description:
First purchased for my oculus GO .And now for Quest 3. This is a must have for space geeks and should be used in schools for its educational value .The Developer has crammed an abundance of information into this amazing experience. The updated version (since my  GO Days) now has free flight leaving the ship and really gives that lost in space feeling.and the later sequences comparing our sun to other stars are mind blowing not to mention the exhilaration when flying through Saturn's rings.

Highly recommend 👌.
It is the kind of game I have always wanted to play. You learn a lot and the immersive feeling of travel is great. Great fun for the kids as well.

I can see that some work went into this, but the beauty of space is about the sheer size not just a bit of text.  Apart from the text, the voiceover is poor and scaling is just wrong.  Seeing the solar system and distance between planets so much out of scale just looks really bad.    As per another reviewer, I can’t see many adults enjoying this.

This could be a great app if:
- you had the ability to see the universe / distance between planets true to scale - as its meant to be.
- being able to fly between planets at a speed of your choosing (ie from speed of sound to multiplications of speed of light) to get a better education of distance and time it would take to travel
- improve the cockpit
- update graphics for Quest 3

Will now try to get my money back…. But will happily pay again for an improved version.

developer_response.body:
Appreciate your feedback!  I get you - I also enjoy hopping into spacecraft to travel where I please. :)

Firstly when you say the "the voiceover is poor" - the app has just been updated to improve exactly that.  No more breathing noises!

To your larger point, the thing about seeing planets and stars at their actual size is that they are just so big that they look flat and 2D.  This app is meant to be a holographic tour, so everything is initially scaled down to 1 millionth of their actual size, with distances intentionally compressed to make it easier to compare their sizes and allow exploration to be more hands-on in order to take advantage of stereoscopic 3D.  The tour guide goes into all this early on.  Plus, later on the tour does give a demonstration of what it's like to see things at accurate distances, mostly to highlight the immense amount of space between solar system bodies.

Hope you found what you're looking for!

reviews.review_description: I boast about this app, "Titans of Space: PLUS," to everyone. I have gotten chills every time I use it. The ability to comprehend the magnitude of the apps showcasing is mind-boggling, to say the least. I get chills.

So obviously it's not a game so you have to be an OG nerd or just enjoy learning to like this, but for cheaper than a ticket to the museum you can get a cooler and more educational tour of the solar system. The blue geek ball serving as the tour guide misspeaks at times and obviously isn't a professional voice actor, but it didn't feel like a detraction to me. It was quaint in a way. I'm just glad someone took the time to make this.

I like the sense of awe VR adds to the experience. I wish there were more space tour type of apps maybe with just a bit more graphical polishing. I'd sit and just stare out into space and wonder for awhile.

This is an incredible app for learning about the solar system at an advanced level. There is the option to just look around and read basic facts along the tour, but the audio option is the highlight. Will bore children and those looking for a quick fly through, perfect for documentary geeks and adults looking for an educational experience.

What a fantastic experience; love the teacher/narrator who does a great job. 
I find myself putting this on when feeling a bit overwhelmed or down and it really helps being able to see our place in the in the galaxy in perspective!
I, perhaps greedily, would love more content eventually which I would happily pay for; maybe constellations or being able to check out some galaxy’s with the teacher.
Great job, bravo 👏👏👏
Unreal how much information is shared by this app.amazing worth every penny!

Where to start?  First and foremost:  the scale issue . Not acceptable ! Second : this seems to have you hard coded into the metric system .  Now , I am a pilot and an electronics engineer so metric is well known to me but it is  being forced here . The system used by Americans , especially pilots is the standard system . No . We ARE with it . YES . We know metric . We are not dumb . We use knots or KIAS in flight  plans , we use inches or mecury not millibars and we use miles not kiloneters . If we want metric , we will convert it . All of my flight conputers ,- even my mechanical E6B can use metric or standatd . I would say that the programers need to get with it and add the option for standard too. I know metric well but my preference  is not to have it forced on me . Third : this goofy. narrator !  nuff said . 
There is very little here of value .  I suppose if you have kindergarten children and you want to intro them to the planets then this could have some value . Frankly it is worthless to anyone else . This is the worst application I have ever purchased . It took me less than 15 minutes to realize it was garbage .
The ONLY bright spot is that it is has multiple languages. I can switch to Dutch and that is nice but that narrator! I would say if you look at the first tron movie and were to pattern your narrator after a graphic that looks less like a CG male reproductive gland and has a better voice . Maybe use the Bit character and get some voice talent from the first Tron might help . There is no way to give this negative stars . Pity. Honestly , I could have cranked out a better application with 90's- Corel Click and play author software . Refund !

I do not usually write a review, however I needed to write one for this. I was so amazed by everything about this, it has blown my mind and will never get tired of floating around the solar system looking at all the planet's and moons. I love the way you can zoom in on a planet and hold it in your hand. Well worth the money! Well done guys

This app is simply a remarkable experience. If you like space, jump into it. You'll not be disappointed. 

Nevertheless, the app could be much better. More stars can easily be included in the Sun to Canis Maoris journey, and visuals may be a bit more improved to make immersion better overall. It'd be lovely see more visual content added, like stars, galaxies and other Celestial objects, rather than narration. Keep up the excellent work!
This has got the best the highest quality science/learning app I’ve ever seen in VR. It’s like a AAA science experience from a AAA science museum. You really have to see it to believe it. Buy this.

Truly an amazing experience, learned a lot from this, worth every penny!! If you want to pursue a career in space, or just think space is cool, this is for you! Comparing planets and other heavenly bodies in VR is a one of a kind experience. 10/10, would definitely buy again

reviews.review_title: Great guided journey through the heavens
reviews.review_description:
You fly around the solar system in your own private spaceship, visiting the planets and major satellites while your friendly, quirky guide gives you a great deal of information. What’s not to like?

The graphics are really very good, some of the best I’ve seen on the Quest platform so far. It is very educational but not stiflingly so. What sets the apart from the other space apps available is the guide. This was my biggest reservation going in, because it looks so cartoony, but his comforting voice and enthusiasm shine through. He really seems to be talking to - you in a very effective, conversational manner. 

It really is great flying through space in this app and I’d thoroughly recommend it for those who are curious about what’s out there. Ideal for children and grown-ups alike.

Informative. Relaxing. Click around your flight deck so you find all the features. I most enjoy listening rather than reading everything through a journey. As others have said it would be nice if updates extended through the Milky Way and to other galaxies.

reviews.review_title: Not worth paying to be honest
reviews.review_description: NZD10 for a 30 minute tour of space (only our solar system) where you can't even control your aircraft. I've watch at least 2 VR360/180 videos on YouTube VR that are probably more immersive and informative. Information about those planets are readily available online anyway more indepth.

reviews.review_title: Great taster of what’s to come
reviews.review_description:
Overall I loved the immersion of being up close to planets in space! 

It was very scary to see the size of the Sun and other stars, and it suddenly makes you realise that there must be a God who created the Universe. 

Only reason this wasn’t 5 stars was due to the graphics, after watching films like the Martian and Gravity, I believe we can go one level further. 

For the price this is a great experience, that I will no doubt share with friends and family. 

Would love to visit the asteroid belt. 

Hats off to the developer.

reviews.review_title: Did you know mars has volcanos?
reviews.review_description: AND ONE IS THE SIZE OF FRANCE! Lol. This game is a fun and engaging learning experience. The visuals are great, and it really puts a perspective on just how small we are in the universe.

I added this to my library and it's among my top picks when demonstrating virtual reality to newbies. Its a great addition to my space collection with ISS and Apollo 11 being other masterpieces. I liked how there's an option to use less motion for 'motion sensitive' people.

If you are only going to get one "experience app" that you can show to your family, get this one. My only problem was that the english narrator audio has not been cleaned from very disturbing and distracting sounds of narrator's breathing. Something that should be easy for the devs to fix, but possibly got overlooked.

developer_response.body: I'm glad you said something!  I actually thought I went through and cleaned those out.  I will get some outside help to identify the rest and address in the next update.

reviews.review_description: If you want to learn about most things space, play this. It is a bit restrictive but it's amazing for the price. Must have for any people who love learning or if you just are bored!
Excellent

I love the premise of a vr space tour and what is there now is spectacular. I know what I was getting into with 30 minutes of gameplay, but I wish there was a bit more. Reminds me of universe simulator. Prehaps options to bring up known exo planets and stars will give this more game time and more educational value

I wish I could give this experience (not really a game...) ten stars. It is without a doubt the best thing on VR. It absolutely blew me away. Fingers crossed there will be DLC content in the future to show us more of the nearest constellations and stars. 

If you want to see what VR is capable of, buy this game.

reviews.review_title: If you love space, you’ll love this
reviews.review_description: Really awesome, pretty much the same feeling I got going to a science center, which even to this day, absolutely enamour me with the things you can learn. Really something special and will show anyone I know that likes space because it’s about a 1/3 of the cost and just as cool as going somewhere. Obviously just a bit about space and our solar system and not about nature, technology and the like, but I think you’ll know what I mean.
Been disappointed in things like this in the past. But not this time. The price is on point, it's visually stunning and seems well made.
I wish there would be more very educational stuff like this

reviews.review_title: Awesome experience of the solar system
reviews.review_description: I used Titans of Space on Android cardboard for the first time and I loved it. When I saw version for Oculus I bought it without thinking and I wasn’t disappointed. Great tour of solar at system including most important moons. I was nicely surprised by being able to get out of the spaceship. Also, nice audio commentary. Good job with availability of other languages. It would be great if you also had audio for those. Overall very recommended.

This has been my favourite experience on the Oculus so far. If you have any interest in space then give this a go. I did the full tour (plus a bit of 0g exploring around a few planets, especially Saturn) but spread over a couple of days.

Such a great idea and executed brilliantly.
This is a great app. Lots of interesting info and the visuals are pretty good. If you have any interest in our solar system you will enjoy this.

Astonishingly beautiful game. It is educational and simply mind blowing. If anything can be added, that’s free movement or at least free movement between planets, etc. And also — galaxies, black holes. But what it is now — already a wonder.
